subject: ela
title: Summarizing a Passage
content:
Summarizing a Passage

What is a Summary?

A summary is a short version of a longer text. It includes only the most important ideas in your own words, in the same order as the original.

Elements of a Good Summary

1. Tells who or what the passage is about
2. Includes only the main ideas (not every detail)
3. Uses your own words
4. Is much shorter than the original (usually 1-3 sentences)
5. Keeps the same order as the original

How to Summarize

Step 1: Read carefully and understand the text
Step 2: Ask — What is this mostly about?
Step 3: Find the key ideas that support this main point
Step 4: Write 1-3 sentences that combine these ideas
Step 5: Check — Does this sound like the original?

What NOT to Include

• Your opinions or feelings
• Minor details
• Examples (unless crucial)
• Exact wording from the passage (unless a quote)

Practice Summary

Original: "On Monday, Sarah woke up at 7 AM. She ate breakfast and brushed her teeth. Then she walked to school. She had math class first. In math, they learned about fractions. After lunch, they had science. Sarah learned that plants need sunlight. She walked home at 3 PM."

Summary: On Monday, Sarah went to school where she learned about fractions and plants. She walked home at 3 PM.

Why This Works

✓ Focuses on main events
✓ Omits routine details
✓ Stays in order
✓ Much shorter than original

questions.prompt: Which of these is the best summary of a story where a boy finds a puppy, names it, and takes it home?
choices: The boy finds a puppy and names it
The boy finds a lost puppy and decides to care for it as his new pet
The boy's mom said no pets
questions.hint: Include the main action and outcome

questions.prompt: What should NOT be included in a summary?
A passage has 8 paragraphs. How long should the summary be?
When summarizing in order, what should you follow?
Should a summary use exact words from the original?
questions.hint: Summaries are in your own words
